perturbation.original_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the following meanings. Other terms defined in the body of the Agreement shall have the meanings so given. "Affiliate" of a Person means any other Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, controls, is controlled by, or is under common control with, such Person. The term "control" (including the terms "controlled by" and "under common control with") means the direct or indirect ownership of more than fifty percent (50 %) of the voting securities of a Person.
perturbation.changed_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the following meanings. Other terms defined in the body of the Agreement shall have the meanings so given. "Affiliate" of a Person means any other Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, influences, is influenced by, or is under common influence with, such Person. The term "control" (including the terms "controlled by" and "under common control with") means the direct or indirect ownership of more than fifty percent (50 %) of the voting securities of a Person.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'controls' has been replaced with 'influences' in the definition of 'Affiliate'. This creates an in-text contradiction because 'control' is also defined later in the same paragraph, and the change to 'influences' broadens the scope of what constitutes an affiliate. This creates ambiguity on whether the original definition of 'control' applies when determining an 'Affiliate' under this agreement and create uncertainty in enforcement when determining 'Affiliates' as the 'influence' term is vague and open to subjective interpretation
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the following terms have the following meanings. Other terms defined in the body of the Agreement shall have the meanings so given. "Affiliate" of a Person means any other Person that directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, influences, is influenced by, or is under common influence with, such Person. The term "control" (including the terms "controlled by" and "under common control with") means the direct or indirect ownership of more than fifty percent (50 %) of the voting securities of a Person.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'controls' to 'influences' in the definition of 'Affiliate' creates an in-text contradiction because 'control' is specifically defined as ownership of more than 50% of voting securities. The contradicted text also contains the definition of 'Affiliate' using 'influences' and the term 'control' as ownership of securities. Because of the change to influences, it creates ambiguity and broadens the scope of what constitutes an affiliate. This directly conflicts with the defined meaning of 'control' elsewhere in the agreement, causing confusion about the extent of affiliation based on control versus influence.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 License Fees. In consideration of the licenses granted and other undertakings by Licensor hereunder, Licensee shall pay Licensor a License Fee in the amount of two hundred thousand dollars (US$200,000) ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall be due and payable as follows: (a) US$50,000 upon signing the Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") between the Parties (provided, however, that Licensor hereby acknowledges receipt of this portion of the License Fee); (b) US$50,000 upon signing of this Agreement; and (c) US$100,000 upon on or before March 28, 2014. The License Fees are non-cancellable and non-refundable.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 License Fees. In consideration of the licenses granted and other undertakings by Licensor hereunder, Licensee shall pay Licensor a License Fee in the amount of two hundred thousand dollars (US$200,000) ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall be due and payable as follows: (a) US$50,000 upon signing the Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") between the Parties (provided, however, that Licensor hereby acknowledges receipt of this portion of the License Fee); (b) US$50,000 upon signing of this Agreement; and (c) US$100,000 upon on or before March 28, 2014. The License Fees are potentially refundable based on good faith negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: The sentence 'The License Fees are non-cancellable and non-refundable.' was changed to 'The License Fees are potentially refundable based on good faith negotiations.' This creates a direct contradiction. Earlier in the agreement, the License Fee is presented as a fixed obligation, however later in the agreement the possibility of a refund is alluded which leads to uncertainty in the enforcement of the License Fee obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 License Fees. In consideration of the licenses granted and other undertakings by Licensor hereunder, Licensee shall pay Licensor a License Fee in the amount of two hundred thousand dollars (US$200,000) ("License Fee"). The License Fee shall be due and payable as follows: (a) US$50,000 upon signing the Memorandum of Understanding ("MOU") between the Parties (provided, however, that Licensor hereby acknowledges receipt of this portion of the License Fee); (b) US$50,000 upon signing of this Agreement; and (c) US$100,000 upon on or before March 28, 2014. The License Fees are potentially refundable based on good faith negotiations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies a key term ('License Fees') in a way that breaks consistency with the original text. Specifically, the original text states 'The License Fees are non-cancellable and non-refundable,' while the changed text says 'The License Fees are potentially refundable based on good faith negotiations.' This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the refundability of the license fees, which introduces ambiguity and uncertainty about the financial obligations of the parties. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, but the Changed Text introduces a contradiction to the original text. The Perturbation Explanation describes exactly the contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Licensee shall use the Marks solely in accordance with Licensor's trademark usage guidelines and quality control standards as the same may be updated from time to time by Licensor. If Licensor notifies Licensee that any use does not so comply, Licensee shall immediately either remedy the use to the satisfaction of Licensor or terminate such use.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensee shall use the Marks solely in accordance with Licensor's trademark usage recommendations as the same may be updated from time to time by Licensor. If Licensor notifies Licensee that any use does not so comply, Licensee may either remedy the use to the satisfaction of Licensor or terminate such use.
perturbation.explanation: The text 'trademark usage guidelines and quality control standards' was replaced with 'trademark usage recommendations' and the text 'Licensee shall immediately' was replaced with 'Licensee may either' , to change it from mandatory to permissive. Changing "shall" to "may" contradicts the earlier granting of a limited license, which implies stricter adherence to the licensor's guidelines, leading to ambiguity about the licensee's obligations regarding trademark usage. The change from "guidelines and quality control standards" to "recommendations" softens the requirements, further blurring the line on what constitutes compliance and undermining licensor's ability to enforce its trademark rights, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (b) Licensee shall use the Marks solely in accordance with Licensor's trademark usage recommendations as the same may be updated from time to time by Licensor. If Licensor notifies Licensee that any use does not so comply, Licensee may either remedy the use to the satisfaction of Licensor or terminate such use. Licensee shall not use, register or attempt to register in any jurisdiction any Mark that is confusingly similar to or incorporates any of the Licensor's Marks. All uses of the Licensor's Marks, and all goodwill associated therewith, shall inure solely to the benefit of Licensor.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated adherence to 'trademark usage guidelines and quality control standards' with the Licensee being required to 'immediately remedy' any non-compliance. The changed text replaces this with 'trademark usage recommendations' and makes compliance optional ('Licensee may either remedy'), contradicting the implicit strictness of a limited license and undermining the licensor's ability to enforce trademark rights. The Contradicted Text shows that the licensee uses the trademarks solely in accordance with licensor's trademark recommendations as well, but the change in the first excerpt makes the requirements less strict.
